name: Hamilton
description: The 2015 Broadway hip-hop musical by Lin-Manuel Miranda, about founding father Alexander Hamilton.

questions.q: Who wrote the book, music, and lyrics for Hamilton?

source.notes: Miranda also originated the title role.

questions.q: Which U.S. founding father's life is the subject of Hamilton?

source.notes: First U.S. Secretary of the Treasury.

questions.q: Hamilton opened on Broadway in which year?

source.notes: Previews began July 13, 2015; opened at the Richard Rodgers Theatre August 6, 2015.

questions.q: What is the name of Alexander Hamilton's wife, a co-protagonist in the show?

source.notes: Elizabeth Schuyler Hamilton, née Schuyler.

questions.q: Which U.S. President acts as a father figure and mentor to Hamilton in the musical?

source.notes: Played originally by Christopher Jackson.

questions.q: Who narrates the musical, introducing himself with the line 'I'm the damn fool that shot him'?

source.notes: Played originally by Leslie Odom Jr.

questions.q: What happens in the musical's climactic duel between Hamilton and Burr in Weehawken, New Jersey?
choices: Hamilton survives but is politically ruined

source.notes: Historically accurate; July 11, 1804.

questions.q: Who originated the role of Aaron Burr on Broadway and won a Tony for it?

source.notes: Won the 2016 Tony for Best Actor in a Musical.

questions.q: Which Tony-winning actor double-cast as Lafayette and Thomas Jefferson in the original production?

source.notes: Won the 2016 Tony for Best Featured Actor in a Musical.

questions.q: Angelica Schuyler was originated by which actress, who won the 2016 Tony for Best Featured Actress?

source.notes: Famous for the showstopping 'Satisfied.'

questions.q: Which actor originated the comic role of King George III, singing 'You'll Be Back'?

source.notes: Brian d'Arcy James was the original off-Broadway King George.

questions.q: Hamilton draws heavily on a 2004 biography of Alexander Hamilton by which historian?

source.notes: Chernow later served as historical consultant to the musical.

questions.q: Before its Broadway run, Hamilton premiered Off-Broadway in 2015 at which theater?

source.notes: Premiered February 2015; transferred to Broadway in August.

questions.q: Who directed the original Broadway production of Hamilton?

source.notes: Kail also directed In the Heights and Fosse/Verdon.

questions.q: Hamilton's first public performance was a single song by Lin-Manuel Miranda at which 2009 event?

source.notes: Miranda performed an early version of 'Alexander Hamilton' for the Obamas.

questions.q: The Act II opener 'What'd I Miss' introduces which character returning from France?

source.notes: Jefferson's entrance in purple velvet.

questions.q: In Act II, Hamilton's affair with which woman leads to the first major American political sex scandal?

source.notes: Chronicled in 'Say No to This' and 'The Reynolds Pamphlet.'

questions.q: Hamilton's filmed Broadway production was released in 2020 on which streaming service?

source.notes: Released July 3, 2020.

questions.q: Hamilton won how many Tony Awards in 2016?

source.notes: Nominated for 16 — a record.

questions.q: Hamilton famously uses a rotating platform during numbers like 'Satisfied' and 'Hurricane'. What's it commonly called?

source.notes: A double concentric turntable designed by David Korins.

questions.q: Hamilton's opening song introduces Alexander as an immigrant born in which Caribbean location?

source.notes: 'A bastard, orphan, son of a whore and a Scotsman' — Nevis, 1755/57.

questions.q: Hamilton won the Pulitzer Prize in 2016 in which category?

source.notes: Only the ninth musical ever to win the Pulitzer for Drama.
